2605.09314.title: How LLMs Are Persuaded: A Few Attention Heads, Rerouted
2605.09314.paper_summary: Language models can be persuaded to abandon factual knowledge. This vulnerability is central to AI safety, but its internal mechanism remains poorly understood. We uncover a compact causal mechanism for persuasion-induced factual errors. A small set of mid-layer attention heads almost entirely determines the model's answer. These heads write answer options into a low-dimensional polyhedron, with options occupying distinct vertices. Persuasion does not blur belief or merely reduce confidence; it causes a discrete latent jump from the correct-answer vertex to the persuasion-target vertex. We show that decision heads are not reasoning over evidence. Instead, they copy whichever option token their attention selects. Persuasion works by redirecting attention. We isolate a rank-one evidence-routing feature that controls the route. Directly modifying this feature steers the model's choice, and removing it blocks persuasion. We then trace the feature back to a band of shallower attention heads that build it from persuasive keywords in the input. Every step is validated by intervention. This mechanism appears across open-source LLMs and realistic poisoning scenarios such as Generative Engine Optimization, revealing persuasion as a narrow, monitorable circuit.
2605.09314.paper_summary_zh: 語言模型可以被說服放棄事實知識。這種脆弱性對於人工智慧安全至關重要，但其內部機制仍然不甚了解。我們揭示了一種緊湊的因果機制，用於說服引起的事實錯誤。一小組中層注意力頭幾乎完全決定了模型的答案。這些頭將答案選項寫入一個低維多面體中，選項佔據不同的頂點。說服並不模糊信念或僅僅減少信心；它導致從正確答案頂點到說服目標頂點的離散潛在跳躍。我們顯示決策頭並不是在基於證據進行推理。相反，它們複製其注意力選擇的任一選項標記。說服通過重新導向注意力來運作。我們隔離了一個一級證據路由特徵，控制著路徑。直接修改這一特徵可以引導模型的選擇，而去除它則阻止說服。然後，我們追溯這一特徵到一組較淺的注意力頭，它們從輸入中的說服性關鍵字構建它。每一步都通過干預進行驗證。這一機制出現在開源LLMs和現實的中毒場景中，例如生成引擎優化，揭示說服是一個狹窄的、可監控的電路。
2605.09314.author: Xiangkun Sun et.al.
2605.09314.authors: Xiangkun Sun, Lingkai Kong, Aoqi Zhang, Liang Zeng, Tonghan Wang
